title: Using the Confocal Microscope with Enterococcus faecalis and NAO dye.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"am0ct","text":"Using the Confocal Microscope with Enterococcus faecalis and NAO dye.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":35,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare an Enterococcus faecalis overnight culture. In 10mL of Brain Heart Infusion (BHI) broth put in 1 colony.
Next morning, dilute overnight E. faecalis to an OD600 of 0.01 in fresh BHI. Supplement as needed.
Check OD600 after 3 hours. Looking for an OD ~0.4.
Isolate 4mL of log phase culture and place in BD Facs tube.
Suspend pellet in 1mL of sterilized 1X PBS.

Stain with NAO dye for 30 minutes in the dark at room temp. Use 7.5uL of a 10-3diluted NAO stock to give stain with 1.5uM NAO. The initial concentration of NAO is 100mg in 1mL of DMSO or 212mM in 1mL of DMSO. Make a 1000 fold dilution in DMSO 1uL → 999uL DMSO This dilution is stable if kept wrapped in tin foil at room temp.
After 30 minutes, flood stained cells with 3mL of 1X PBS.
Suspend pellet in 4 mL of 1X PBS.
Prepare slides with a poly-L-lysine fixative
Add 9uL of stained cells to the fixative.
Add a drop of Anti-Fade reagent
Place a coverslip. Use Kim wipes on both sides of the slide and apply pressure to reduce the amount of floating bacteria.
Seal coverslip with VALAP. Smear it around the edges. Heat up Vaseline, Lanolin, and Parafin
creator: Eadewunm
